name: Chinese Country Ribs & Hill Country Ribs
recipeIngredient: 1 T Cornstarch: dissolved in 1 T cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-1/2 quart slow cooker, combine ketchup, honey, vinegar, soy sauce, five-spice powder, onion, ginger, and garlic.
2. Position a broiler rack 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the broiler.
3. Broil the ribs, turning once, until browned (about 10 minutes).
4. Transfer the ribs to the slow cooker. Stir to coat the ribs with the sauce.
5. Cover and slow cook until the ribs are tender (5-6 hours on LOW).
6. Transfer the ribs to a platter and cover with aluminum foil to keep warm.
7. Skim the fat from the surface of the sauce.
8. In a medium saucepan, bring the sauce to a simmer over medium heat.
9. Cook until reduced to about 1 cup (6-8 minutes).
10. Stir in the cornstarch mixture and cook just until thickened.
11. Pour the sauce over ribs and serve immediately with hot cooked rice.
HILL COUNTRY RIBS:
Follow steps 1-5 above.
To thicken the sauce, skim the fat from the surface, pour into a medium saucepan, and simmer until reduced to 1 cup.
